title: Distance on the Coordinate Plane
why: Finding distance between two points runs on subtraction and counting steps along a line. Warm up subtracting whole numbers and counting gaps on a number line, and distance on the grid becomes "just subtract."

diagnostic.q: How many steps from 2 to 7 on a number line?
To find the distance between 3 and 8, you can ___.

learn.heading: Distance means subtract
learn.intro: The distance between two points on a line is how many steps apart they are. When they line up across or up, you subtract the smaller number from the bigger one.
examples: Same row: a point at x = 1 and a point at x = 6 are 6 − 1 = 5 apart.

tiers.intro: Subtract one step at a time.

items.q: C. How many steps from 0 to 6?

tiers.intro: Count the gap between two numbers.

items.q: A. How many steps from 2 to 9?
B. Two points sit at x = 1 and x = 5. How far apart are they?

tiers.intro: Stretch into distance on a grid.

items.q: A. Points (3, 2) and (8, 2) are in the same row. How far apart are they?
items.hint: Same y, so subtract the x-values: 8 − 3 = 5.

items.q: B. Points (4, 1) and (4, 6) are in the same column. How far apart are they?
items.hint: Same x, so subtract the y-values: 6 − 1 = 5.

exit.q: 2. Points (2, 5) and (9, 5) are in the same row. How far apart are they?
